skill.name: zach-feature-demand-validator
skill.description: Validates whether an Amazon product feature is real customer demand using review, keyword, and community evidence.
skill.summary: Validate Amazon feature demand with review signals, keyword data, and public community discussion.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a high volume of command, filesystem, network, cryptography, and obfuscation patterns. Manual review found no confirmed malicious intent: most command findings are markdown examples, network access is disclosed public research, and scripts perform local parsing and CSV generation. The skill is safe to publish with low operational risk from its intentional web research and local file processing.

low_findings.title: False positive: markdown command references
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported many Ruby or shell backtick executions. Review showed these are markdown inline code spans and fenced examples in documentation, not executable Ruby backticks or shell execution in code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are markdown documentation and the matching syntax is used for inline code or command examples. No executable Ruby file or runtime backtick execution path was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False positive: PowerShell usage examples
low_findings.description: PowerShell invocations are present as Windows user instructions for running the local Python utilities. They do not execute automatically and do not contain download, credential, or persistence behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched PowerShell appears only inside fenced documentation blocks. The commands call repository Python scripts with local input and output paths.

low_findings.title: Low risk: public web research endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill intentionally uses WebSearch and WebFetch for Amazon review pages, Google Trends, Reddit, and Quora research. The reviewed URLs are public data sources or examples, not hidden exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is disclosed in the skill instructions and maps to the stated product research function. No evidence found of secret collection or transmission to unrelated services.

low_findings.title: Low risk: local file parsing and output generation
low_findings.description: The Python scripts read user-provided JSON, CSV, TXT, HTML, and manifest files, then write CSV deliverables. This filesystem access is core functionality, but users should run it only on trusted review exports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code performs direct local parsing and validation without subprocess calls or network transmission. Arbitrary user-selected paths are allowed by design, so this remains a minor operational risk.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography and credential markers
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography, certificate, and key-file patterns, but targeted review found no md5, sha1, encryption, private key, token, password, or credential handling in the skill source files. The likely matches are words such as keyword, validation, and source metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted searches for cryptographic and credential APIs found no supporting code in the reviewed source. The cited contexts are documentation or command-line metadata fields.

low_findings.title: False positive: obfuscation heuristics
low_findings.description: High-entropy and dangerous-combination heuristics appear to be triggered by dense multilingual markdown, tables, examples, and declared tool capabilities. No prompt injection text, encoded payload, or obfuscated execution chain was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are human-readable markdown and Python. The dangerous combination is explained by legitimate WebSearch, WebFetch, Bash, and file parsing capabilities declared for the skill.

content.value_statement: Amazon sellers risk building features that buyers do not value. This skill validates feature demand with review evidence, keyword signals, and public community discussion.
actual_capabilities: Validates a product feature with review, keyword, and community evidence.
Parses Sorftime product review JSON into feature-focused CSV rows.
Parses local review_source_pack folders with CSV, TXT, Markdown, or HTML exports.
Generates standard keyword and community signal CSV files from structured JSON inputs.
Checks final report folders for required markdown, CSV files, columns, and review evidence.
Documents fallback research paths using WebSearch, WebFetch, Google Trends, Reddit, and Quora.
limitations: Best results require Sorftime MCP access for review and keyword data.
Fallback web research has lower precision than Sorftime data.
It validates existing evidence and does not make final product investment decisions.
Local review packs must contain trustworthy exports with valid ASIN metadata.

use_cases.title: Screen a feature before tooling
use_cases.description: Validate whether buyers mention, search for, or discuss a proposed micro-innovation before committing manufacturing budget.

use_cases.title: Compare competitor feature gaps
use_cases.description: Check whether a competitor difference represents real customer demand or only a design choice with weak evidence.

use_cases.title: Prioritize listing claims
use_cases.description: Identify feature claims supported by reviews, keywords, and community language before writing a product listing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic demand check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zach-feature-demand-validator to check whether steam is real demand for air fryers in the US market. Summarize review, keyword, and community signals.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation for one category and one feature.

prompt_templates.title: ASIN-based validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate whether self-cleaning is a meaningful feature for ASIN B0PUBLIC01. Compare review evidence from that ASIN with nearby competitors and note data gaps.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a target product and wants direct feature evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Fallback without Sorftime
prompt_templates.prompt: I do not have Sorftime access. Use the fallback path with public web research and my local review_source_pack. Generate the standard CSV evidence files and mark source precision clearly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user needs a complete workflow without MCP data.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive feature decision
prompt_templates.prompt: A competitor added a wattage display to a USB cable. Validate whether this is real demand or a weak novelty using reviews, keyword signals, and Reddit or Quora discussions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced competitive analysis for roadmap prioritization.

output_examples.input: Validate steam as a feature for air fryers in the US market.
output: Review signal: Customers mention dry food and moisture problems in relevant reviews.
Keyword signal: Searches for steam air fryer terms show measurable interest.
Community signal: Public discussions ask about texture and moisture outcomes.
Verdict: Treat as a possible true demand and test cost impact before tooling.

output_examples.input: Check whether a wattage display on USB cables is worth copying.
output: Review signal: Some users value charging visibility, but complaint volume is limited.
Keyword signal: Search language exists but may be niche.
Community signal: Discussion appears technical and concentrated among power users.
Verdict: Use as a secondary differentiator, not the main purchase claim.

output_examples.input: Run validation using a local review_source_pack only.
output: Review pack parsed into standard review evidence rows.
Keyword and community dimensions require public web fallback sources.
Report should label lower precision and include source links or query terms.
Verdict should remain tentative until all three dimensions have evidence.

best_practices: Define the feature with three to five clear English keyword variants before collecting evidence.
Use real ASIN values and keep source links or query terms for every CSV row.
Treat demand as strong only when review, keyword, and community signals align.
anti_patterns: Do not use placeholder ASIN values such as B0XXXXX or UNKNOWN.
Do not infer demand from one positive review cluster without keyword or community support.
Do not mix Sorftime and fallback data without labeling the source precision difference.

faq.question: What does this skill validate?
faq.answer: It validates whether a specific Amazon product feature has evidence of real buyer demand.

faq.question: Does it require Sorftime?
faq.answer: Sorftime gives the strongest workflow, but the skill documents fallback paths using public search and local review packs.

faq.question: What files can a review_source_pack contain?
faq.answer: It can contain CSV, TXT, Markdown, or HTML review exports with a source_manifest.json file.

faq.question: Can it make the final product decision?
faq.answer: No. It provides evidence and a demand judgment, but humans must decide cost, risk, and roadmap priority.

faq.question: What deliverables does it create?
faq.answer: It expects a markdown validation report and five standard CSV files for review, keyword, trend, extended keyword, and community signals.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
